Title: GT61 β-1,2-xylosyltransferases define a conserved xylan modification in gymnosperm and Arabidopsis primary cell walls

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Xylan biosynthesis / primary cell wall hemicellulose modification
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: side-route / decorating step on xylan backbone
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: xylan backbone with glucuronic acid-spaced acceptor sites; UDP-xylose inferred donor
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: β-1,2-xylosylated xylan motif yielding XUXY2XX after GH11 digestion
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Likely transfers xylose in β-1,2 linkage onto xylan backbone positions two residues away from GlcA substitutions
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Likely contributes to primary cell wall architecture and xylan–cellulose interaction
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: primary wall-rich tissues, especially PEM and needles
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target structural xylan modification
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: High expression correlates with accumulation of GH51-resistant, GH62-sensitive β-1,2-xylosylated primary wall xylan motif
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Specifically strong expression in pro-embryogenic masses; lower in other tissues
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ddPCR expression profiling correlated with xylan oligosaccharide fingerprinting and phylogenetic assignment
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: ddPCR expression analysis; no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PrGT61-IIIA is a Group III GT61 highly enriched in PEM, a primary cell wall-rich tissue. 2. Its expression coincides with accumulation of XUXY2XX, the GH51-resistant and GH62-sensitive β-1,2-xylosylated xylan oligosaccharide. 3. It was prioritized as the candidate conifer enzyme for primary wall xylan β-1,2-xylosylation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential marker/candidate for engineering primary wall xylan architecture in conifers
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Candidate identified by expression–structure correlation; not directly biochemically assayed in this paper

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Xylan biosynthesis / primary cell wall hemicellulose modification
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: side-route / decorating step on xylan backbone
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: UDP-xylose donor and deacetylated xylan acceptor, especially low-acetylated glucuronoxylan
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: β-1,2-xylosylated xylan motif yielding XUXY2XX, XY2XX, and Y2XXXUX diagnostic products after enzymatic digestion
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers xylosyl residues in β-1,2 linkage onto xylan backbone at a preferred position two xylose residues away from glucuronic acid substitutions
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: β-1,2-xylosylated xylan motif in primary wall xylan
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Likely supports even-patterned xylan compatible with cellulose interaction and primary wall architecture
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: primary cell walls; detected in conifer primary wall-rich tissues and engineered Arabidopsis stem walls
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target structural xylan modification
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Introduces evenly spaced β-1,2-xylosyl side chains on xylan, generating the conserved GlcA–Xyl motif characteristic of primary wall xylan
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key enzyme for this specific side-chain installation, not stated as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Active when heterologously expressed in Arabidopsis esk1 kak8; marginal in WT and gux1 gux2
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Low acetylation favors activity; glucuronic acid spacing influences product pattern
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana microsomes; in vitro enzyme assay with UDP-xylose and xylan acceptor; heterologous expression in Arabidopsis; PACE; GH11/GH115/GH30/GH3/GH62 digestions; MALDI-CID MS; subcellular localization
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: IRX3 promoter (secondary-wall specific) in Arabidopsis; 35S promoter for GFP localization in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PmXYXT2 localizes to the Golgi and acts as a xylan β-1,2-xylosyltransferase. 2. In vitro and in planta it generates GH62-sensitive, GH51-resistant xylosylated xylan products. 3. It places the β-1,2-xylosyl branch with strong positional specificity two residues from GlcA, producing the even-patterned Y2XXXUX / XUXY2XX motif. 4. Activity is strongest on low-acetylated xylan, particularly in esk1 kak8 Arabidopsis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful tool to engineer primary wall xylan patterning and cellulose-compatible hemicellulose architecture; likely best deployed in low-acetylation backgrounds
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Limited activity in normal acetylated secondary walls; possible wall architecture effects if ectopically expressed
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Orthologous to Pinus taeda PtXYXT2 previously shown to encode β-1,2-xylosyltransferase activity

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: side-route / decorating step on xylan backbone
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: β-1,2-xylosylated xylan products including XUXY2XX and XY2XX
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers xylosyl residues in β-1,2 linkage onto xylan backbone with more flexible positioning relative to GlcA than PmXYXT2
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to xylan branching but with heterogeneous substitution pattern
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: engineered Arabidopsis stem walls; inferred conifer xylan
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target xylan branch structure
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces β-1,2-xylosylated xylan but without strict spacing relative to GlcA, creating more heterogeneous products
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Most active in gux1 gux2, weak in esk1 kak8, inactive in WT Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Activity reduced by GlcA and acetylation in secondary wall xylan contexts
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Microsomal assay and heterologous expression in Arabidopsis followed by enzymatic fingerprinting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PmXYXT1 is a β-1,2-xylosyltransferase from GT61 Group II. 2. It generates both XUXY2XX and XY2XX, unlike the stricter PmXYXT2. 3. Its branching pattern is more flexible and heterogeneous relative to GlcA positioning. 4. It is most active in the GlcA-deficient gux1 gux2 background.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May be useful when dense or heterogeneous xylan xylosylation is desired rather than precise patterning
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Less positional specificity may create complex wall structural effects
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as a comparative core enzyme to distinguish Group II versus Group III specificity

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Xylan biosynthesis / primary cell wall hemicellulose modification
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: side-route / decorating step on xylan backbone
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: UDP-xylose donor and xylan acceptor, favored in low-acetylation context
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: β-1,2-xylosylated xylan motif yielding XY2XX and Y2XXXUX/XUXY2XX diagnostic products
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers xylose in β-1,2 linkage onto xylan backbone, generating a regularly spaced branch two residues from GlcA substitutions
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Likely contributes to primary wall architecture, mucilage adherence, and wall remodeling during ageing
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: callus primary walls; seed coat mucilage; engineered stem secondary walls
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target structural xylan modification
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Encodes an Arabidopsis xylan β-1,2-xylosyltransferase that generates the same conserved GlcA-linked xylosylated motif found in conifers
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Predominant in seed coat; elevated later in leaf development/senescence; detectable in primary wall-rich callus inferred
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In transgenic esk1 kak8 stem under IRX3 promoter, produces strong β-1,2-xylosylation signal; endogenous product enriched in callus
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Activity reduced in normal acetylated secondary walls; low acetylation favors activity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous/ectopic expression in Arabidopsis; enzymatic fingerprinting; GH30/GH3/GH62 analyses; MALDI-CID MS; mutant analysis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome data mentioned for senescence expression, no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MUCI21/MUM5 is directly validated as a xylan β-1,2-xylosyltransferase and renamed AtXYXT1. 2. It introduces GH62-sensitive, GH51-resistant β-1,2-xylosyl branches on xylan. 3. It generates the same evenly spaced Y2XXXUX / XUXY2XX motif seen in conifer and Arabidopsis primary walls. 4. Its single knockout only partially reduces the product, indicating redundancy with AtXYXT2 and AtXYXT3.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for modifying seed mucilage adherence and primary wall xylan architecture; should consider redundancy with AtXYXT2/3
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutation causes mucilage adherence defect and influences developmental/senescence traits
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously implicated in mucilage branching; this study provides direct enzymatic evidence that it modifies xylan rather than requiring alternative substrate models

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Xylan biosynthesis / primary cell wall hemicellulose modification
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: side-route / decorating step on xylan backbone
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: β-1,2-xylosylated xylan motif contributing to Y2XXXUX
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acts redundantly with AtXYXT1 and AtXYXT3 to add β-1,2-linked xylosyl side chains to xylan
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Likely contributes to primary wall architecture and developmental/stress-associated wall remodeling
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target structural xylan modification
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Single loss has little effect, but combined loss with AtXYXT1/3 progressively reduces and ultimately abolishes β-1,2-xylosylated xylan
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly induced during heat stress according to cited transcriptome data; expressed during later leaf development
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Heat stress induction mentioned in discussion
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA knockout and double/triple mutant genetic analysis with GH30/GH3 oligosaccharide profiling
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome data mentioned, no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtXYXT2 is one of the three Arabidopsis GT61 genes required redundantly for β-1,2-xylosylated xylan production. 2. Single and double mutant analyses show it contributes quantitatively but is not solely responsible. 3. Along with AtXYXT1 and AtXYXT3, it is necessary for the Y2XXXUX primary wall xylan motif.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Must be stacked with AtXYXT1/3 for complete editing of the trait; may be useful in stress-responsive wall engineering
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious seed mucilage defect alone; broader developmental roles possible
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional assignment is genetic/redundant rather than direct enzyme assay in this paper

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Xylan biosynthesis / primary cell wall hemicellulose modification
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: side-route / decorating step on xylan backbone
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: β-1,2-xylosylated xylan motif contributing to Y2XXXUX
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acts redundantly with AtXYXT1 and AtXYXT2 to add β-1,2-linked xylosyl side chains to xylan
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Likely contributes to primary wall architecture and senescence-associated wall remodeling
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target structural xylan modification
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required redundantly with AtXYXT1 and AtXYXT2 for complete synthesis of β-1,2-xylosylated xylan; triple loss abolishes the motif
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Elevated in later leaf development/senescence according to transcriptome data
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR-Cas9 mutagenesis in atxyxt1 atxyxt2 background and oligosaccharide profiling
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome data mentioned, no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtXYXT3 is the third redundant Arabidopsis GT61 required for β-1,2-xylosylated xylan formation. 2. CRISPR disruption in the atxyxt1 atxyxt2 background abolishes Y2XXXUX. 3. It contributes to the conserved primary wall xylan modification together with AtXYXT1 and AtXYXT2.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential editing target to fully remove the xylan β-1,2-xylosylation trait in Arabidopsis-like systems
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Triple mutant shows delayed senescence and altered rosette phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No homozygous T-DNA knockout available; assigned through CRISPR-based genetic evidence
